summary: SAT Integration feature testing completed. All backend API endpoints and frontend UI components working correctly.

GET /api/sat/status.description: Returns SAT integration status for the company
GET /api/sat/status.notes: Returns configured=false when no credentials, configured=true with RFC and status after saving

POST /api/sat/credentials.notes: Validates RFC (12-13 chars), CIEC (min 8 chars), converts RFC to uppercase, supports upsert

GET /api/sat/comprobante-types.description: Returns list of CFDI comprobante types
GET /api/sat/comprobante-types.notes: Returns 6 types: todos, ingreso, egreso, pago, nomina, traslado

DELETE /api/sat/credentials.notes: Returns success when credentials exist, 404 when no credentials

sat_integration_card.description: SAT Integration card appears in CFDI module
sat_integration_card.notes: Card shows 'Integración SAT' title, description, RFC status, and action buttons

configure_credentials_dialog.description: Configure Credentials dialog opens with RFC/CIEC fields
configure_credentials_dialog.notes: Dialog has RFC input (with placeholder), CIEC password input (min 8 chars hint), security note, and Guardar/Cancelar buttons

save_credentials.description: Save credentials button works and shows success message
save_credentials.notes: Shows 'Credenciales SAT guardadas correctamente' toast, updates RFC display

delete_credentials.notes: Shows confirmation dialog '¿Eliminar credenciales SAT?', after deletion shows 'No configurado' status

edit_credentials.description: Edit button opens dialog with existing RFC
edit_credentials.notes: Allows updating RFC and CIEC credentials

critical_code_review_comments: SAT portal web scraping (SATPortalClient) is implemented but cannot be fully tested without real SAT credentials - this is expected behavior
Credentials are properly encrypted using Fernet encryption before storage
SAT_ENCRYPTION_KEY should be set in production .env for consistent encryption
context_for_next_testing_agent: SAT Integration feature fully implemented and tested. Backend endpoints: GET /api/sat/status, POST /api/sat/credentials, GET /api/sat/comprobante-types, DELETE /api/sat/credentials. Frontend: SATIntegration.js component in CFDIModule.js page. The actual SAT portal sync (POST /api/sat/sync) requires real RFC+CIEC credentials and cannot be tested in this environment.

mocked_apis.SAT Portal Web Scraping: The actual SAT portal login and CFDI download (SATPortalClient class) is implemented but CANNOT be tested without real SAT credentials. The credential storage, status, and delete endpoints work correctly.

notes: The SAT integration uses Selenium for web scraping the SAT portal. The actual sync functionality requires valid RFC and CIEC credentials from the Mexican tax authority (SAT). All credential management features (save, status, delete) are working correctly with encrypted storage.
